title: salvinia auriculata
intro: Salvinia auriculata, also known as ear cabomba or water wisteria, is a floating aquatic plant native to South America that belongs to the Salvinia genus. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Salvinia auriculata include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and treating digestive problems. Salvinia auriculata is often prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used as a compress or poultice to apply its medicinal properties topically.
